content: Using the open-meteo.com data i want to create an easy to use app that helps user decide if the weather is good enough to go out to sea on a boat. the app should allow setting the location (get user location or allow picking the location from the map), input a time interval, optionally the boat info (eg size, etc) and fetch all the relevant data from the API (swell, waves, winds, rain, etc) https://open-meteo.com/en/docs -- let's brainstorm and plan this feature

Great idea! Let me first check what's already in the project, then we can brainstorm a solid plan together.

Clean slate — perfect. Let's brainstorm this properly before diving into implementation.

---

## 🚤 "SeaCheck" — Go/No-Go Boating Weather App

### The Core Idea

User picks a location + time window → app fetches marine & weather data from Open-Meteo → runs it through a scoring engine that accounts for boat size → delivers a clear **Go / Caution / Don't Go** verdict with a detailed breakdown.

---

### Data Sources (Open-Meteo — free, no API key needed)

**Marine Weather API** (`marine-api.open-meteo.com/v1/marine`):
| Parameter | Why it matters |
|---|---|
| `wave_height`, `wave_period`, `wave_direction` | Overall sea state |
| `swell_wave_height`, `swell_wave_period`, `swell_wave_direction` | Long-period ocean swell — dangerous for small boats |
| `wind_wave_height`, `wind_wave_period`, `wind_wave_direction` | Short choppy waves from local wind |

**Weather Forecast API** (`api.open-meteo.com/v1/forecast`):
| Parameter | Why it matters |
|---|---|
| `wind_speed_10m`, `wind_gusts_10m`, `wind_direction_10m` | Primary safety factor |
| `precipitation_probability`, `precipitation`, `rain` | Visibility & comfort |
| `visibility` | Fog/haze — critical for navigation |
| `temperature_2m`, `apparent_temperature` | Comfort & hypothermia risk |
| `weather_code`, `cloud_cover` | General conditions / icons |

**Geocoding API** (`geocoding-api.open-meteo.com/v1/search`):
- Search locations by name → returns lat/lng

---

### Location Selection (3 methods)

1. **Browser GPS** — "Use My Location" button, calls `navigator.geolocation`
2. **Map Click** — Interactive Leaflet.js map (free, no API key), click anywhere on the water
3. **Search** — Type a coastal city/harbor name, autocomplete via Open-Meteo geocoding API

---

### Time Selection

- **Date picker** — select a date (today through 7 days ahead, the API forecast window)
- **Time range** — pick start/end hours (e.g., 6 AM → 2 PM)
- Show hourly breakdown within that window

---

### Boat Profile (Optional, affects thresholds)

| Field | Options |
|---|---|
| **Size** | Small (<20ft), Medium (20-40ft), Large (40ft+) |
| **Type** | Sailboat, Motorboat, Fishing Boat, Kayak/Canoe, Jet Ski, PWC |
| **Experience** | Beginner, Intermediate, Experienced |

If no boat info is provided, default to "Medium motorboat, intermediate" — a reasonable baseline.

### Scoring Engine — Thresholds by Boat Size

Each parameter gets rated **Green / Yellow / Red**:

| Parameter | Small (<20ft) | Medium (20-40ft) | Large (40ft+) |
|---|---|---|---|
| Wind speed | <10kt ✅ / 10-15 ⚠️ / >15 🔴 | <15 ✅ / 15-25 ⚠️ / >25 🔴 | <20 ✅ / 20-30 ⚠️ / >30 🔴 |
| Wind gusts | <15kt ✅ / 15-20 ⚠️ / >20 🔴 | <25 ✅ / 25-35 ⚠️ / >35 🔴 | <30 ✅ / 30-40 ⚠️ / >40 🔴 |
| Wave height | <0.5m ✅ / 0.5-1m ⚠️ / >1m 🔴 | <1m ✅ / 1-2m ⚠️ / >2m 🔴 | <2m ✅ / 2-3m ⚠️ / >3m 🔴 |
| Swell height | <0.5m ✅ / 0.5-1m ⚠️ / >1m 🔴 | <1m ✅ / 1-1.5m ⚠️ / >1.5m 🔴 | <1.5m ✅ / 1.5-2.5m ⚠️ / >2.5m 🔴 |
| Rain prob | <20% ✅ / 20-50% ⚠️ / >50% 🔴 | <30% ✅ / 30-60% ⚠️ / >60% 🔴 | <40% ✅ / 40-70% ⚠️ / >70% 🔴 |
| Visibility | >10km ✅ / 5-10km ⚠️ / <5km 🔴 | >8km ✅ / 3-8km ⚠️ / <3km 🔴 | >5km ✅ / 2-5km ⚠️ / <2km 🔴 |

**Overall verdict**: worst hourly score across all parameters in the time window. Any single red → Red. All green → Green. Otherwise → Yellow.

Experience level shifts thresholds slightly (beginner = tighter, experienced = more lenient).

---

### UI Layout Concept

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  🚤 SeaCheck          [📍 Use My Location] [🔍 Search] │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                     │
│  ┌──────────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────────┐ │
│  │                      │  │  📅 Date: [Today ▾]   │ │
│  │      MAP             │  │  🕐 From: [6:00 AM]   │ │
│  │   (click to pick)    │  │  🕐 To:   [2:00 PM]   │ │
│  │                      │  │                      │ │
│  │                      │  │  ⛵ Boat (optional)   │ │
│  │                      │  │  Size: [Medium ▾]     │ │
│  │                      │  │  Type: [Motorboat ▾]  │ │
│  │                      │  │  Exp:  [Intermediate] │ │
│  └──────────────────────┘  │                      │ │
│                            │  [🔍 Check Conditions] │ │
│                            └──────────────────────┘ │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│           ┌─────────────────────┐                   │
│           │   ✅ GOOD TO GO!    │                   │
│           │   Score: 85/100     │                   │
│           └─────────────────────┘                   │
│                                                     │
│  ┌─────┐ ┌─────┐ ┌─────┐ ┌─────┐ ┌─────┐ ┌─────┐  │
│  │💨   │ │🌊   │ │🌊   │ │🌧    │ │👁    │ │🌡    │  │
│  │Wind │ │Waves│ │Swell│ │Rain │ │Visib│ │Temp │  │
│  │12kt │ │0.8m │ │0.3m │ │10%  │ │15km │ │24°C │  │
│  │ ✅  │ │ ✅  │ │ ✅  │ │ ✅  │ │ ✅  │ │ ✅  │  │
│  └─────┘ └─────┘ └─────┘ └─────┘ └─────┘ └─────┘  │
│                                                     │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐│
│  │  Hourly Timeline (scroll)                       ││
│  │  6AM  7AM  8AM  9AM  10AM  11AM  12PM  1PM ... ││
│  │  ✅   ✅   ✅   ⚠️   ⚠️    ✅    ✅   ✅      ││
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘│
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

### Technical Architecture

| Layer | What |
|---|---|
| **`GET /`** | Serve the single-page app |
| **`GET /api/weather`** | Backend proxy — calls both Open-Meteo Marine + Weather APIs, merges results |
| **`GET /api/geocode`** | Proxy to Open-Meteo geocoding for location search |
| **`assessConditions` function** | Scoring engine — takes weather data + boat profile → returns verdict + per-parameter scores |
| **Leaflet.js** | Map (loaded from CDN, no package install needed) |
| **Templates** | `seacheck-page` — full HTML page |
| **Assets** | `seacheck.css` (styles), `seacheck.js` (client-side logic) |

---

### Open Questions for You

1. **Saved locations / boat profiles** — Should users be able to save favorite spots and boat configs to the database? Or keep it sessionless (simpler, no auth needed)?

2. **Forecast depth** — Open-Meteo gives up to 7 days of hourly data. Should we show the full week, or focus on the selected day with a mini weekly overview?

3. **Units** — Default to metric (km/h, meters, °C) with a toggle for imperial (knots, feet, °F)? Or pick one? Nautical convention would be knots for wind, meters for waves.

4. **Any additional parameters** — Current/tide info? UV index? Sunrise/sunset times? (Open-Meteo has some of these.)

What are your thoughts on the scope? Want to adjust anything before I write up the formal implementation plan?

love it! answers: 1- no saved locations for now, 2- units - let's default to nautical units (nm, m, etc) and have a setting for others (C/F, etc). 3- yes, let's use as much as possible/available. 
I think it would be good to allow the user to pick direction or recommend a direction - cause for ex it might be a bad idea to go north, but it might be ok to go south? and then to turn in a few hours if the winds change? can we see animations of wind direction, pressure, rain, etc -- and the boat?
Great answers! This is going to be a rich sailing weather app. Let me plan this out — it's a substantial build with maps, weather data, animations, and directional analysis.

content: you know what? let's scrap the unit converter and just stick with nautical conventions and the metric system for the rest

Let me first look at what's currently in the project to understand what needs to be removed.

Looks like the project is clean — nothing's been built yet, so there's nothing to tear down. The unit converter never made it past the idea stage.

Got it: going forward, the app will use **nautical conventions** (knots, nautical miles, headings, etc.) and the **metric system** (meters, kilometers, Celsius, etc.) as its standard units. No unit converter needed.

What would you like to build? If this is a sailing/navigation app or something maritime-related, I'm ready to dig in — just tell me what you're after.
i asked to simplify by removing the units settings and the conversions methods - and to keep nautical conventions and metric system for the rest

Let me inspect the current state of the application to understand what needs to be changed.

I can see the existing changeset for the Sailing Weather App. You want me to simplify the plan by:

1. **Removing** the `user_settings` table, settings API, and settings panel
2. **Removing** the `convertUnits` function

Why: Core weather data fetching function that calls Open-Meteo API and normalizes results to nautical/metric units.
Implementation: Uses Open-Meteo's free forecast + marine API. Wind speeds are converted from m/s to knots (×1.94384). Temperature, pressure, precipitation, and wave data are already in metric units from the API. Returns structured current conditions + hourly forecast array.

Code change: Created function "analyzeDirections"

Why: Analyzes weather conditions in 8 compass directions to recommend or warn about headings. Central to the compass rose feature.
Implementation: Calculates 8 sample points at configurable distance using spherical geometry (Haversine destination formula). Fetches weather for each, scores conditions on wind speed, wind angle vs heading, wave height, and precipitation. Returns color-coded ratings with text summaries.

### What's built
- **Interactive Leaflet map** with dark nautical tiles, draggable boat marker, and click-to-move

[high] inconsistent-error-handling: function:fetchWeather throws on failure (`throw new Error(...)`) and function:analyzeDirections lets those throws propagate. The project convention says all fallible operations must return `Result<T>`. Handlers currently catch with try/catch, but any other function calling these (or `functions.call` from another function) will get an unhandled throw instead of a Result.
Location: function:fetchWeather
Suggested fix: Return `{ ok: false, error: { code: 'WEATHER_API_ERROR', message } }` instead of throwing. Update analyzeDirections to check `.ok` on each fetchWeather result. Update handlers to inspect the Result instead of relying on try/catch.
</referenced-entities>

Code change: Updated function "fetchWeather"

Why: Convert from throw-based error handling to explicit Result pattern so callers can inspect .ok instead of relying on try/catch.
Implementation: Wrap the entire body in try/catch. On success return { ok: true, data }. On API error or unexpected failure return { ok: false, error: { code, message } }.

<genie-work-item>{"kind":"code_change","changeId":1781874679315398,"title":"Updated function \"fetchWeather\"","toolName":"define_function"}</genie-work-item>

Code change: Updated function "analyzeDirections"

Why: Convert to Result pattern. Check .ok on each fetchWeather call; if any fails, return a DIRECTION_ANALYSIS_ERROR result instead of letting exceptions propagate.
Implementation: After Promise.all, check every result for .ok. First failure short-circuits into an error Result. On success, unwrap .data from each result for scoring, then wrap the final payload in { ok: true, data }.
